Program_Description: Medi-Cal is California’s Medicaid healthcare program that provides comprehensive health coverage for people with low or no income.
Target_Population: Pregnant individuals meeting income thresholds; varies by state expansion status
Eligibility_Requirements: To qualify for Medi-Cal, you have to live in California and meet program eligibility rules.

Program_Description: WIC provides nutritional support for women, infants, and children.
Target_Population: Low-income pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women; infants and children

Program_Description: Parents as Teachers is a program that provides parent educator home visits.
Target_Population: Expectant parents and families with children from birth through age 3 (or 5 in some cases).

Program_Name: Alliance for Innovation on Maternal Health
Program_Description: The Alliance for Innovation on Maternal Health focuses on maternal safety and quality improvement in obstetric care.
Target_Population: Birthing hospitals and obstetric providers; indirectly serves all pregnant women.

Program_Description: The Comprehensive Perinatal Services Program (CPSP) is a Medi-Cal benefit that provides a wide range of culturally competent services to pregnant women from conception through 60 days postpartum.
Target_Population: Pregnant women from conception through 60 days postpartum

Program_Description: Nurse-Family Partnership provides home visiting services by registered nurses to first-time, low-income pregnant women.
Target_Population: First-time, low-income pregnant women enrolled by 28 weeks gestation

Program_Description: Parents as Teachers is a program that provides home visits and support for expectant parents and families with young children.
Target_Population: Expectant parents and families with children from birth through age 3 (or 5 in some cases)

Program_Description: WIC provides nutritional support and education for low-income pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women, as well as infants and children.
Target_Population: Low-income pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women; infants and children

Program_Description: Comprehensive nursing assessments, education and linkage to community resources for pregnant women identified as being at-risk for substance use, depression, and domestic violence.
Target_Population: Pregnant women identified as being at-risk for substance use, depression, and domestic violence.

Program_Description: Program aimed at providing support and education to expectant parents and families with children.
Target_Population: Expectant parents and families with children from birth through age 3.

Program_Description: This program is designed to address and enhance the health, social, economic, and educational well-being of expectant and parenting adolescents/youth and their children in California.
Target_Population: Expecting and/or Parenting female and male who is 21 years old or younger residing in Ventura County
Eligibility_Requirements: Expecting and/or Parenting female and male who is 21 years old or younger

Program_Description: The WC program assists pregnant women, new parents, and infants or children under the age of five with nutrition education, vouchers for healthy supplemental foods, finding healthcare and accessing other community and social services.
Target_Population: Low-income pregnant women, new parents, and infants or children under the age of five

Program_Description: WIC is a health and nutrition program for growing families that provides nutrition education, breastfeeding support, vouchers for healthy foods, and referrals to healthcare and other community services.
Target_Population: Low-income pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women; infants and children
Eligibility_Requirements: To find out if you are eligible for WIC services use the online eligibility assessment.
Application_Process: To enroll or change an appointment, call or email.

Program_Description: Nurse-Family Partnership (NFP) is a free program that supports first-time moms build healthy futures for themselves, their babies, and their families.
Target_Population: First-time moms (28 weeks gestation or less) facing social, financial, or environmental challenges.
Eligibility_Requirements: Ventura County resident; facing social, financial, or environmental challenges that may affect health or well-being.
Application_Process: Anyone can refer to NFP; pregnant individuals can refer themselves or be referred by a healthcare provider or community agency.
